title: MC: Reinspired
description: Giving life to all the gameplay Mojang forgot
body:
**Dedicate at least 7GB of RAM for the best experience!**

## Minecraft: Reinspired

Using my free will I made a Minecraft modpack, please enjoy!

Ultimately, this whole project answers one question of mine:
> _"How would I have done it?"_

This pack overhauls progression, enchanting, redstone, transportation, exploration, structures, and more!

This whole modpack was essentially a test to see how much of the gameplay I could change without adding any new blocks/mobs. At some point I went past that test, but I think you can see that original idea reflected in the sheer number of systems I've messed around with to make this pack.

If any of these following features resonate with you, I think you’ll really appreciate what this pack has to offer;

- Slow but meaningful progression for deeper engagement
- No need for excessive grinding to access features
- Fair alternatives to typically RNG reliant elements
- Thoughtful mechanics that encourage strategic gameplay
- Inventory management that prefers challenge over frustration
- Thoughtful integration of the create mod's progression & automation
- Plenty of quality-of-life improvements and client-side enhancements
- Support for all play styles, whether you're playing solo or with friends

## Current Development

This modpack is in **Alpha** and currently only supports singleplayer. I hope to figure out server support soon.

I also have plans to add advancements that guide you along the pack's progression, keep an eye out for that!

For now though, you can contact me in the [discord server](https://discord.gg/zx5e7XcUGr) if you have any questions <3

<details>
<summary>Modpack Credits</summary>
  
- You!

</details>
<details>
<summary>Incompatible Mods</summary>
  
- Leather Processing mod doesn't include water bucket from the Ceramic Bucket mod.

</details>
<details>
<summary>Recommended Experience</summary>
  
- Play on Hard mode, please! There is balancing for all difficulties, though.
- Avoid map mods, you're supposed to get lost until you find tools to navigate.
- This pack is created to be built upon, and should work with most mods.
- You can add fabric mods, but you have to test which ones work.

</details>
